G6PD Deficiency 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/g6pddeficiency.html 
G6PD Deficiency
Also called: Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency 
Summary 
Glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) deficiency is a genetic disorder that is most common in males. About 1 in 10 African American males in the United States has it. G6PD deficiency mainly affects red blood cells , which carry oxygen from the lungs to tissues throughout the body. The most common medical problem it can cause is hemolytic anemia .
